title: sambucus nigra var. virescens
intro: Sambucus nigra var. virescens, also known as green elder, is a variety of the black elder tree, a shrub native to Europe and North Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to contribute to its various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating cold and flu symptoms, and supporting the immune system. Green elder berries can be prepared as a tea, syrup, or tincture, and are also used in jams and other food products, although it's essential to note that the berries and other parts of the plant should be properly identified and prepared before consumption.
